name: Ronit Raj
title: Full-Stack AI & ML Engineer
tagline: Full-stack AI & ML engineer who likes shipping - from training and serving models (GRPO on AMD MI300X, vision-language RAG) to the full-stack apps and infra around them. 9x hackathon podiums, a startup, and GitMesh - a Linux Foundation (LFDT) project I maintain.
credentials: SIH 2024 Winner · NHA PM-JAY Runner-Up · OSS India 2026 Speaker · GitMesh Maintainer

availability.message: Open to Full-Stack AI / ML Engineer roles and startup collaborations.

about.summary: I build because I can't not - give me a hard problem and a weekend and I'll have something running. That took me from a static HTML site in 2019 to fine-tuning LLMs on AMD MI300X and shipping production AI that holds up under real traffic and messy data. Along the way: 9 hackathon wins, a startup, and GitMesh - an LFDT AI-agent governance project I maintain. Now focused on full-stack AI and AI-infra - turning demos into systems people depend on.

sections.title: Where It Started
paragraphs: In 2019 I shipped my first website - a static hotel landing page with responsive layout, a mobile side nav, and vanilla JavaScript (still on GitHub as first-website). No framework, no backend - just HTML, CSS, and curiosity. That foundation is the through-line to today: PARAM on Base Sepolia, GitGambit over the GitHub API, PM-JAY pipelines on Gemma, and a 40-minute session at OSS India 2026.

sections.title: My Journey in AI
paragraphs: I started with a simple question at IIIT Gwalior: how do you make complex systems actually work? That curiosity grew into building production AI - from military intelligence platforms to on-chain applications. Winning Smart India Hackathon 2024 with a 79%-accuracy RAG system taught me the lesson that shapes everything I build: great AI isn't fancy architectures, it's shipping systems that hold up in production.

sections.title: The Infra Underneath
paragraphs: The part I keep coming back to is the infrastructure beneath the model. I built InferenceGym, an RL environment that treats LLM serving as a control problem - batching, KV-cache budgets, speculative decoding, prefill/decode splits - and trained a policy that beats hand-tuned heuristics. I've fine-tuned with GRPO and LoRA on AMD MI300X, deployed models as Docker Spaces on Hugging Face, and built RAG pipelines over Qdrant and FAISS. I've shipped on-chain systems too - academic credentials, a micropayment CLI - when decentralization actually earns its place. But what pulls me in is making inference fast, cheap, and reliable at scale.

sections.title: From Hackathons to Production
paragraphs: Build fast, ship faster, iterate. Co-founding Garudex Labs taught me the gap between demo and production is where engineering happens. Whether shipping EdTech serving 50+ students or maintaining open-source tools like GitMesh, the real work is error handling, feedback loops, and making sure the system doesn't break when real users hit it.

sections.title: Technical Approach
paragraphs: PyTorch and Transformers for the models; FastAPI and Next.js for delivery; whatever serving stack keeps latency and cost sane. I reach for smart contracts only when decentralization adds real value. Mostly I optimize for one thing: code other engineers can read, deploy, and extend - because the best system is the one that ships and keeps working when I'm not watching it.
